scope_principles.rule: Route shell access is not action authority.
scope_principles.source: permissionEngine.evaluateRouteBoundary separates routeShellAccessible, actionDecision and payloadDecision.

scope_principles.rule: Action authority is not payload visibility.
scope_principles.source: permissionEngine.can and visibility tests deny internal payloads even when route or role exists.

scope_principles.rule: Object-scoped actions require explicit subject.objectId and context.objectScope.
Cross-tenant and actor-tenant mismatches fail closed before payload access.
Admin and Security govern access, but cannot bypass advisor, compliance, evidence sufficiency, release, visibility or export gates.

allowed_patterns: VIEW client-visible released recommendation/document/evidence for own tenant
Interact with client-safe surfaces after compliance release
object_scope_required_for: non-view mutations on DECISION, DOCUMENT, EVIDENCE_RECORD, EXPORT_REQUEST, RECOMMENDATION

allowed_patterns: VIEW client-safe tenant data where released
UPLOAD or edit document/data surfaces only when explicit service contract grants it
VIEW released entity-scoped material when object scope matches
VIEW explicitly released client-visible payload
VIEW or collaborate on explicitly scoped document surfaces when released/allowed

role_scope_matrix.canonical_scope: Own tenant internal workbench and draft preparation
allowed_patterns: VIEW internal advice payload for assigned tenant
REVIEW internal signals/evidence where service contract grants it
blocked_patterns: Evidence sufficiency approval if not compliance role
role_scope_matrix.proof_anchor: internalAdvicePayloadRoles includes analyst; release/approval gates exclude analyst

role_scope_matrix.canonical_scope: Own tenant advisor review and approval, not client release
allowed_patterns: APPROVE recommendation when object scope matches
VIEW internal advice payload for own tenant

role_scope_matrix.canonical_scope: Own tenant compliance release, block, evidence sufficiency and export approval
allowed_patterns: RELEASE or BLOCK recommendation when object scope matches
APPROVE evidence sufficiency when object scope matches
APPROVE restricted export package when object scope matches
VIEW internal advice payload for own tenant

role_scope_matrix.canonical_scope: Tenant onboarding and data follow-up, not advice payload
allowed_patterns: INVITE tenant/user when scoped
Support tenant setup and data follow-up
role_scope_matrix.proof_anchor: tenantAdminRoles includes client_success; internal advice payload denies client_success

allowed_patterns: MANAGE roles and policies
ASSIGN/REVOKE permissions
INVITE tenant/user
Govern platform setup
blocked_patterns: Internal advice payload by route authority alone

role_scope_matrix.canonical_scope: Security controls, second confirmation and access governance, non-bypass only
allowed_patterns: MANAGE sensitive role/policy controls
ASSIGN/REVOKE access
INVITE where tenant/user invitation is security-governed
blocked_patterns: Internal advice payload by route authority alone

object_scope_requirements.rule: For non-VIEW actions on these object types, both subject.objectId and context.objectScope are required.

denials.meaning: A mutation-like action was requested without a concrete target object.
A concrete target exists but the current session/context has no explicit matching object scope.
The scoped object type differs from the requested subject object type.
The scoped tenant differs from the requested subject tenant.
The requested object id is outside the current object scope.

bold_cleanup_recommendations.recommendation: Make CurrentUserContext the canonical input for all new P0 journey/process APIs; only adapt to DemoSession at a narrow boundary where legacy services still require it.
bold_cleanup_recommendations.legacy_removed: Stops roleKey/tenantSlug query params from acting like real identity.

bold_cleanup_recommendations.recommendation: Deprecate createDemoSession in safety-critical tests; require tryCreateDemoSession or requireDemoSession so bad role/tenant inputs cannot silently fall back.
bold_cleanup_recommendations.legacy_removed: Removes hidden Bennett/compliance fallback from proof paths.

bold_cleanup_recommendations.recommendation: Separate route, action, payload and object-scope assertions in every P0 permission test.
bold_cleanup_recommendations.legacy_removed: Prevents route visibility from being mistaken for client payload access.

bold_cleanup_recommendations.recommendation: Name Admin/Security explicitly as governance and non-bypass roles, never as superuser release roles.
bold_cleanup_recommendations.legacy_removed: Kills the broad admin-can-do-anything mental model.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Every demo role is mapped to canonical scope and blocked patterns.
Object-scope requirements are explicit for protected object types.

overclaim_guard: BD-02-B is a role/object-scope matrix, not a completed negative access proof suite. The denial test plan is BD-02-C.
